classifyString.name: Classify a string
classifyString.description: Write helper functions to check if a string is alpha, numeric, or alphanumeric, then use them to classify the input string.

duck.name: Duck
duck.description: A purely alphabetic string should be classified as "Alpha".

number.description: A purely numeric string should be classified as "Numeric".

alphanumeric.name: Duck42
alphanumeric.description: A string with both letters and numbers should be classified as "Alphanumeric".

notAlphanumeric1.name: It's not 42!
notAlphanumeric1.description: A string with special characters should be classified as "Unknown".

notAlphanumeric2.name: 42 Rubber Duck!
notAlphanumeric2.description: A string with spaces and special characters should be classified as "Unknown".

whereToStart.question: Where should I start when there are several functions to write?
whereToStart.answer: Pick the smallest helper first. A `contains()` function (which asks 'does this character appear in this string?') is the foundation that everything else can build on.

checkAllLetters.question: How do I check if every character in the input is a letter?
checkAllLetters.answer: Use `contains()` against the alphabet `"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"`. Loop through each character of the input and ask 'is this one in the alphabet?'. If any aren't, it isn't all alpha.

checkAllDigits.question: How do I do the same check for digits?
checkAllDigits.answer: Exactly the same pattern, but check against `"0123456789"` instead of the alphabet.

alphanumericMeaning.question: What does 'alphanumeric' mean. Does it mean both?
alphanumericMeaning.answer: Alphanumeric means each character is either a letter OR a digit. So for each character, if it's alpha you're happy, and if it's numeric you're also happy. Use `continue` to skip on. Only if it's neither should you reject.

pickingLabels.question: How do I pick between the four labels in whatAmI()?
pickingLabels.answer: Call all three helpers and use if/else. The order matters. Check the stricter cases ('alpha only' and 'numeric only') before 'alphanumeric', which is the looser case.
